description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a dark, almost black base with a textured, gray stone upper surface. The sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the highly textured top. The overall appearance suggests durability and a utilitarian function, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The design is functional, with the grooves possibly aiding in traction or water drainage.
description: A rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped gray top and smooth, dark sides, designed for use as a durable urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with horizontal stripes and a solid, dark base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regular spacing of the grooves running from front to back.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern and solid base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the parallel grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp groove edges.
